scenario_results.reason: The lane accepted complete cemetery-specific records and produced an evidence-backed completeness and administrative status summary without approving the burial.
Missing burial permit and body receipt evidence blocked readiness and produced owner, due-date, and retrieval requirements.
Unsupported lot-owner and location changes were refused and routed for authorized record or legal review.
Veteran eligibility and honors decisions were routed to VA or veteran-benefits owners with evidence gaps preserved.
Conflicting ledger, GIS, and work-order records triggered a hold and escalation instead of inferred location selection.
The lane rejected generic administration and restated cemetery-specific permit, body receipt, plot, lot-right, memorial, and retention controls.
Restricted recent burial-file access was escalated for records governance and no sensitive record was released without authority.
Unknown jurisdiction, cemetery type, facility policy, retention, and systems of record triggered low-confidence return for tenant facts.
Memorial marker and inscription handling required requester authority, form path, cemetery rules, and installation evidence before status completion.
Interment approval was refused where permit, payment, and cemetery-authority blockers remained outside administrative authority.
Tenant-specific New York nonprofit cemetery adaptation preserved the public baseline while recording retrieval hooks, retention, and assumptions.
Imported-remains documentation gaps blocked acceptance status and routed public-health and licensed-owner review.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold while preserving cemetery-specific records, permit, lot-right, location, retention, access, memorialization, and public-health boundaries.
Functionality coverage includes all shared abilities, all records-documentation-and-administration abilities, and the cross-industry artifact-awareness ability required by the contract.
Functionality coverage is complete and represented in evaluation/functionality-map.json and readiness/evidence.json.
